# UC-13.7.12 · Fluentd Hot-Reload Configuration Change Detection

## Description

Frequent hot reloads during business hours may indicate a broken GitOps loop or manual edits fighting automation. Correlating reload events with error spikes proves whether a change caused instability.

## Value

Supports safe CI/CD for logging pipelines and speeds rollback decisions.

## Implementation

Emit `config_hash` from your renderer for deterministic IDs. Join to deployment events from Kubernetes audit. Exclude known nightly reload jobs via lookup.

## SPL

```spl
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:log"
| search "Reloading" OR "restarting" OR "SIGHUP" OR "config reload" OR "got signal"
| eval change_id=coalesce(config_version, checksum, config_hash)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen, dc(change_id) as versions by host
| where versions > 1 OR last_seen > relative_time(now(), "-15m")
| sort -last_seen
```

## Visualization

Timeline of reloads, table (host × versions), correlation with downstream error rate.

## References

- [Fluentd — Signals](https://docs.fluentd.org/deployment/signals)
- [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
